bullets: Cheap Korean offers (Hanwha, Lotte) have become the lowest in the market, dragging PP down and undercutting Chinese and domestic supply.
Producers keep cutting to move volume — Hyosung Vina trimmed yarn again — yet domestic homo is still seen as too high to sell.
PP yarn H550 led the day with a steep drop; buyers stay cautious amid the falling crude backdrop.
PE keeps sliding as Saudi, Chinese and Russian sellers all reset offers sharply lower — no floor in sight.
Russian Sibur cargo saw especially deep cuts; domestic LDPE and LLDPE continue to fall quickly.
HDPE is the lone bright spot, holding firmer on thin ready-delivery inventory.
PVC is drifting lower rather than falling, with competition high but feedstock pressure milder than PP/PE.
Cheap US far-delivery cargo set a new market low, but October arrival means buyers aren't chasing it.
Local producers are holding July offers firm to negotiate deals rather than discount for volume.

summary: Sabic Saudi's July CIF bids fell sharply vs last month; Petrochina LLDPE -10 to 1,010-1,020 CIF.
Russian Sibur cut PE offers hard (HDPE Film -3,400, HDPE yarn -3,800 VND/kg vs two weeks ago).
HDPE holding firmer than LDPE/LLDPE on thin ready inventory (Qamar F1, PTT 5000S still high).
paras: Today, June 23, a distributor of Sabic Saudi Arabia reported the bid-taking price for imported PE on a July-delivery shipment to Vietnamese buyers, CIF from Singapore. This month's bid-taking price fell sharply compared with the offer and closing prices of the previous month.

Specifically, Sabic's LDPE Film was offered at 1,300 USD/ton/CIF; the LDPE coating bid-taking price was 1,460 USD/ton/CIF; the bid-taking price for LLDPE Film grades was all at 1,110 USD/ton/CIF; the bid-taking price for LLDPE injection and roto grades was 1,250 USD/ton/CIF; mLLDPE C6 was at 1,280 USD/ton/CIF; the HDPE Film bid-taking price was 1,150-1,180 USD/ton/CIF depending on grade; the HDPE blow bid-taking price was 1,180 USD/ton/CIF; and the HDPE injection bid-taking price was 1,150-1,200 USD/ton/CIF depending on grade.
On Chinese goods, today the closing price of Petrochina's LLDPE Film to Vietnamese buyers was recorded at 1,010-1,020 USD/ton/CIF, down 10 USD/ton from the closing price late last week.

Another distributor offered LLDPE Film MI 2 grades without additives from Chinese manufacturers to Vietnam at sharply reduced prices versus the prior week. Specifically: Baofeng's LLDPE Film 7042 was offered at 1,080 USD/ton/CIF – down 40 USD/ton week-on-week; Ningmei's LLDPE 7042 for July delivery was offered at 1,036 USD/ton/CIF; Yulong's LLDPE 7042 for July delivery was offered at 1,095 USD/ton/CIF; and Kunlun's LLDPE 7042N was offered at 1,095 USD/ton/CIF – down a sharp 62 USD/ton week-on-week.

On Russian goods, a distributor of Sibur Russia today offered many PE grades to Vietnamese buyers, goods at Hai Phong port and quoted in VND. The offer prices dropped significantly versus two weeks ago. Sibur offered LLDPE Film MI 2 with additives at 1,100 USD/ton/CFR Hai Phong; quoted in VND, this was 32,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, down 2,200 VND/kg from two weeks ago.

The mLLDPE offer was at 33,500 VND/kg incl. VAT, down 2,300 VND/kg from two weeks ago. HDPE Film grades were offered at 33,100 VND/kg incl. VAT, down a sharp 3,400 VND/kg versus two weeks earlier; HDPE yarn was offered at 31,800 VND/kg incl. VAT, down 3,800 VND/kg after two weeks; HDPE blow was offered at 34,400 VND/kg incl. VAT, down 1,800 VND/kg; and HDPE injection was offered at 33,700-34,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, down 1,500 VND/kg versus two weeks earlier. In addition, Sibur also offered some near and off grades. Customers buying large volumes of four containers or more will receive an additional new promotional policy.

Domestically, the downtrend in PE has yet to show signs of stopping, and many grades continue to fall quickly. Early this week, LDPE Film of Chinese origin was commonly offered around 43,000 VND/kg incl. VAT; LDPE Film of Middle Eastern origin such as Sabic, Tasnee and Borouge was commonly offered around 44,000 VND/kg incl. VAT; and LDPE Film 260GG of Lotte Titan Malaysia origin was offered retail at 34,500 VND/kg incl. VAT – down about 1,000 VND/kg from late last week.

On LLDPE, the offer price for LLDPE Film today was commonly recorded in the range of 33,000-34,500 VND/kg incl. VAT. Notably, a large distributor offered Sabic's LLDPE Film 218WJ at 33,500 VND/kg incl. VAT at Hai Phong port; some commercial sellers offered at 34,500 VND/kg incl. VAT to retail buyers around the Hanoi area. In the low-price segment, China's LLDPE Film 7042/7042N was offered at 33,000 VND/kg incl. VAT but recorded transaction prices of only around 32,000 VND/kg incl. VAT. This new price level is down 500-1,000 VND/kg from the prior week, depending on grade and supplier.

HDPE has held prices more firmly than LDPE and LLDPE over the past few weeks. A fairly large distributor in the north offered Qamar's HDPE Film F1 at 36,000 VND/kg excl. VAT, and at 38,000 VND/kg incl. VAT. Ready-delivery inventory of this grade at commercial sellers' warehouses is limited. Another grade with little ready-delivery inventory that is also holding high prices is PTT Thailand's HDPE yarn 5000S, recorded with a supplier offering at 39,000 VND/kg incl. VAT in the south and 39,300 VND/kg incl. VAT in the central region.

summary: Korean Hanwha now cheapest in market: HJ500 at 990 CIF (deal possible at 970); copo BJ300 1,020.
Domestic HJ500 offered at just 30,000 VND/kg incl VAT — ~2,000 below NSRP/Chinese/Russian homo.
Hyosung Vina cut yarn another 500 to 34,700, still considered too high to sell; NSRP 114G to 32,000.
paras: Today, June 23, a distributor offered Hanwha Total Korea's PP injection HJ500 to Vietnam at 990 USD/ton/CIF, and Hanwha's PP block copo BJ300 at 1,020 USD/ton/CIF. There is word that a domestic buyer received a price that could close a deal on the above HJ500 at 970 USD/ton/CIF for a quantity of 198 tons.

Hanwha's offer prices above are nearly the lowest in the market and more competitive than offers for PP of Chinese origin. Domestically, one commercial seller offered PP HJ500 at just 30,000 VND/kg incl. VAT for a shipment arriving early July, accepting further negotiation. This is about 2,000 VND/kg cheaper than PP homo of NSRP Vietnam, Chinese or Russian origin. Hanwha's PP copo BJ300, BJ350 and BJ550 were also recorded at offers of around 33,000-34,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, lower than domestic PP copo offers from Hyosung or other origins.

Besides Hanwha, Korean-origin PP has recently been offered to Vietnam more widely and has seen the steepest price drops. Among these, Lotte's PP yarn H5300 was sold to Vietnamese buyers at 1,030-1,040 USD/ton/CIF, applied to customers buying large volumes.

On Chinese goods, the volume of import offers from suppliers remains quite thin and scattered, not as active as 2-3 weeks ago. Today, a distributor offered Petrochina's PP yarn L5E89 to Vietnam at 1,105 USD/ton/CIF – down 50 USD/ton from the prior week; however, another domestic buyer received an offer for Petrochina's PP yarn at 1,075 USD/ton/CIF, showing a fairly large gap in offer prices among different suppliers. Domestically, PP homo of Chinese origin is commonly offered from 32,000-34,000 VND/kg incl. VAT depending on grade, origin and supplier.

On domestic goods, early this week NSRP's PP 114G was offered lowest at 32,500 VND/kg incl. VAT; the lowest closing deal was at 32,000-32,200 VND/kg incl. VAT depending on quantity. This price is down 300-500 VND/kg from the prior week.

In the south, the Hyosung Vina plant cut its PP yarn offer by a further 500 VND/kg from late last week, down to 34,700 VND/kg incl. VAT in the south. Today, Hyosung Vina quoted PP injection J700N at 32,000 VND/kg excl. VAT (with full 8% VAT, about 34,560 VND/kg incl. VAT); offered PP block copo J640N at 35,000 VND/kg excl. VAT (with full 8% VAT, about 37,800 VND/kg incl. VAT); and offered PP random R701N at 43,000 VND/kg excl. VAT (with full 8% VAT, about 46,440 VND/kg incl. VAT).
Even so, this price level for PP homo is still considered too high and hard to sell domestically.

summary: Shintech US SE950/SE1000 cut a further 20 to 670 CIF, but Oct arrival keeps buyers uninterested.
Coal-based Erdos/Yihua SG-5 21,200-21,300 incl VAT, down 300-400 on week; oil-based H66/266RT 22,000.
TPC Vina July SG660/SP660 21,600-22,032 incl VAT; AGC PM66R 19,700-20,000 ex-VAT; both negotiating.
paras: Today, June 23, a distributor announced the selling price of Shintech USA's PVC SE950/SE1000 to Vietnamese buyers at 670 USD/ton/CIF, far-delivery goods expected to arrive in October 2026. Earlier in mid-month, the closing price of Shintech PVC on a shipment expected to arrive in September was at 690 USD/ton/CIF. Thus, this week's offer price fell a further 20 USD/ton.

Domestically, PVC SE950/SE1000 for immediate delivery is being commonly offered commercially in the range of 22,200-22,500 VND/kg incl. VAT, essentially flat versus the prior week. According to sources, although US imports have fallen in price and are at nearly the lowest level in the market, because the goods are far-delivery, buyers are not too eager to bid.

Yesterday, a large distributor offered PVC SG-5 (coal-based) of Erdos China origin at 21,300 VND/kg incl. VAT at Hai Phong port; another distributor offered PVC SG-5 of Yihua China origin at 21,200 VND/kg incl. VAT at Yen Vien station (Hanoi). These offer prices fell 300-400 VND/kg from the prior week.

On oil-based PVC, also in the north, a distributor offered CGPC Taiwan's PVC H66 at 22,000 VND/kg incl. VAT; the offer for AVT's PVC 266RT (Vinythai Thailand plant, part of AGC Group) was also at 22,000 VND/kg incl. VAT – down 200 VND/kg from last week's lowest price.

Vietnam's two PVC producers, AGC Vina and TPC Vina, both offered PVC selling prices on a July 2026-delivery shipment last week. TPC's PVC SG660/SP660 was offered at 20,200-20,400 VND/kg excl. VAT depending on quantity; with full 8% VAT, that works out to 21,600-22,032 VND/kg incl. VAT. AGC Vina (ACVN) offered PVC PM66R at 19,700-20,000 VND/kg excl. VAT depending on quantity. Into early this week, both plants are holding prices to focus on negotiations and closing orders.
This development shows PVC prices this week moving from flat to slightly down, with high competition.
